metadata.title: Foundational AI Research Papers Timeline
metadata.description: A curated list of the most influential AI research papers that drove the technological revolution.

papers.title: ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
papers.description: The seminal AlexNet paper that brought deep learning back into prominence by winning the ILSVRC-2012 competition with a large convolutional neural network trained on GPUs.

papers.title: Efficient Estimation of Word Representations in Vector Space
papers.description: Introduced the Word2Vec algorithm, fundamentally changing NLP by demonstrating that word embeddings capture syntactic and semantic regularities.

papers.title: Playing Atari with Deep Reinforcement Learning
papers.description: The first paper to successfully apply deep learning to reinforcement learning, showing a single agent learning multiple Atari games directly from pixels.

papers.title: Generative Adversarial Nets
papers.description: Introduced the GAN framework, pitting a generative model against a discriminative model, revolutionizing the generation of highly realistic synthetic images.

papers.title: Dropout: A Simple Way to Prevent Neural Networks from Overfitting
papers.description: Introduced Dropout, a highly effective technique to prevent overfitting in deep neural networks by randomly omitting units during training.

papers.title: Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
papers.description: Introduced the attention mechanism for neural machine translation, allowing the model to focus on relevant parts of the source sentence during translation.
key_contributions: Joint learning of alignment and translation

papers.title: Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image Recognition
papers.description: Introduced VGGNet, proving that increasing the depth of convolutional networks using very small convolution filters (3x3) leads to significant improvements in recognition.

papers.title: Sequence to Sequence Learning with Neural Networks
papers.description: Introduced the encoder-decoder architecture using multi-layered LSTMs, fundamental for all subsequent seq2seq applications.
key_contributions: Reversing source sentences to improve optimization

papers.title: Going Deeper with Convolutions
papers.description: Introduced Inception (GoogLeNet), a deep convolutional architecture that uses 'Inception' modules to perform multi-scale processing while remaining computationally efficient.

papers.title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization
papers.institution: University of Amsterdam / University of Toronto
papers.description: Introduced Adam, an algorithm for first-order gradient-based optimization of stochastic objective functions. It has become the default optimizer for training deep neural networks.
key_contributions: Invariant to diagonal rescaling of the gradients

papers.title: Batch Normalization: Accelerating Deep Network Training by Reducing Internal Covariate Shift
papers.description: Introduced Batch Normalization, allowing for much higher learning rates and less careful initialization by normalizing layer inputs.
key_contributions: Faster and more stable training of deep networks

papers.title: U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation
papers.description: Introduced the U-Net architecture, consisting of a contracting path to capture context and a symmetric expanding path that enables precise localization. It became the gold standard for medical image segmentation.
key_contributions: Data augmentation for small medical datasets

papers.title: You Only Look Once: Unified, Real-Time Object Detection
papers.description: Introduced YOLO, the first real-time object detection system that treats detection as a single regression problem, directly from image pixels to bounding box coordinates and class probabilities.
key_contributions: Global reasoning on images avoiding false positives in backgrounds

papers.title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
papers.description: Introduced ResNet, which solved the vanishing gradient problem in extremely deep networks using skip connections, becoming the default architecture for many vision tasks.

papers.title: Mastering the game of Go with deep neural networks and tree search
papers.description: The landmark paper demonstrating the first computer program capable of defeating a world champion Go player by combining deep neural networks with advanced tree search.
key_contributions: Hybrid AI system combining deep neural networks with Monte Carlo Tree Search
Introduction of policy and value networks for move selection and evaluation
Combination of supervised learning and reinforcement learning (self-play)
Proof of viability for combining deep learning with advanced search at scale

papers.title: Outrageously Large Neural Networks: The Sparsely-Gated Mixture-of-Experts Layer
papers.description: Introduced a Sparsely-Gated Mixture-of-Experts layer, paving the way for massive scale networks with sublinear compute requirements.
key_contributions: Massive capacity increase without proportional compute increase

papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.description: The foundational paper for modern LLMs. It introduced the Transformer architecture, dispensing with recurrence entirely in favor of self-attention mechanisms.

papers.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
papers.description: Introduced PPO, an RL method that strikes a balance between ease of tuning, sample complexity, and performance. It became the default RL algorithm used in RLHF for large language models.

papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
papers.description: Introduced a novel way to pre-train deep bidirectional representations, breaking records across numerous NLP tasks and forming the backbone of Google Search.

papers.title: Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners
papers.description: Introduced GPT-2, demonstrating that language models could perform surprisingly well in zero-shot settings across a variety of domains simply by scaling up parameters and data.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.description: Provided empirical evidence that language model performance scales predictably with compute, dataset size, and parameter count, laying the foundation for modern LLM scaling strategies.
key_contributions: Compute vs. Data vs. Parameter tradeoffs

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
papers.description: Introduced RAG, a hybrid architecture combining a pre-trained sequence-to-sequence model with a dense vector index of Wikipedia for enhanced factual accuracy.
key_contributions: Integration of parametric and non-parametric memory
End-to-end training of retriever and generator

papers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
papers.description: Introduced GPT-3 (175B parameters), showing that massive scaling leads to emergent few-shot learning capabilities without requiring gradient updates or fine-tuning.

papers.title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
papers.description: Popularized high-quality image synthesis using diffusion models, connecting diffusion probabalistic models to denoising score matching. This started the diffusion generative revolution.
key_contributions: High-fidelity image generation surpassing GANs in diversity

papers.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
papers.description: Introduced CLIP, an extremely effective method for contrastive text-image pretraining that enables zero-shot image classification and robust vision-language integration.
key_contributions: Zero-shot transfer to many image classification tasks
Bridging visual and textual modalities effectively

papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
papers.description: Introduced LoRA, freezing the pretrained model weights and injecting trainable rank decomposition matrices into the architecture, greatly reducing the number of trainable parameters.
key_contributions: Massive reduction in VRAM required for fine-tuning
Same or better performance compared to full fine-tuning

papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
papers.description: Introduced the Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) technique, which dramatically reduces the number of trainable parameters for fine-tuning large models.

papers.title: Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold
papers.description: Detailed AlphaFold 2, a revolutionary AI system fundamentally solving the 50-year-old grand challenge of predicting a protein's 3D structure from its amino acid sequence.
key_contributions: Evoformer architecture combining evolutionary and spatial information

papers.title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
papers.description: Introduced Latent Diffusion Models (LDM), which perform the diffusion process in a compressed latent space rather than pixel space, enabling high-quality image synthesis like Stable Diffusion with far less compute.
key_contributions: Compression into latent space via VQ-GAN

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
papers.description: Demonstrated that prompting LLMs to generate a series of intermediate reasoning steps significantly improves performance on complex reasoning tasks.
key_contributions: Emergent reasoning abilities in large-scale models

papers.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
papers.description: Introduced InstructGPT, applying Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) to align language models with user intent. This paper forms the basis for ChatGPT.

papers.title: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
papers.description: Introduced the 'Chinchilla' scaling laws, proving that previous LLMs were significantly undertrained. It showed that model scale and training tokens must scale equally.
key_contributions: 70B model outperforming 280B model through more data

papers.title: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention with IO-Awareness
papers.description: Introduced an IO-aware exact attention algorithm that uses tiling to significantly reduce memory reads/writes between GPU HBM and SRAM, accelerating Transformers immensely.

papers.title: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention with IO-Awareness
papers.description: A critical breakthrough in attention mechanisms that uses tiling and recomputation to speed up Transformers and handle longer sequences.

papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
papers.description: Introduced Constitutional AI, enabling the training of non-evasive and harmless assistants using AI-generated feedback based on a precise set of principles (a 'constitution').

papers.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
papers.description: Released a family of highly capable, open-weight foundation models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters. This paper ignited the massive open-source LLM revolution.

papers.title: GPT-4 Technical Report
papers.description: Detailed the capabilities and safety properties of GPT-4, a large multimodal model reaching human-level performance on many professional and academic benchmarks.
key_contributions: First production-ready multimodal large language model

papers.title: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
papers.description: Introduced DPO, an algorithm that optimizes language models to adhere to human preferences without requiring a complex, separate reward model or RL framework.
key_contributions: Mathematical equivalence of reward models and policies

papers.title: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
papers.description: Introduced DPO, a simpler and more stable alternative to RLHF for aligning LLMs with human preferences without an explicit reward model.

papers.title: Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
papers.description: Released Llama 2, including versions up to 70B parameters, which were optimized for dialogue and released with a permissive license, significantly impacting the commercial use of open models.

papers.title: BitNet: Scaling 1-bit Transformers for Large Language Models
papers.description: Introduced a highly memory and compute-efficient architecture utilizing 1-bit quantization for linear projections, paving the way for running massive LLMs on edge devices.

papers.title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
papers.description: Proposed a new architecture based on Selective State Space Models that achieves linear scaling and rivals Transformers in efficiency.

papers.title: Gemini: A Family of Highly Capable Multimodal Models
papers.description: Introduced the Gemini family of models, designed from the ground up to be natively multimodal, reasoning across text, images, video, and audio simultaneously.
key_contributions: Ultra, Pro, and Nano model tiers

papers.title: The Llama 3 Herd of Models
papers.description: Released Llama 3, pushing the boundaries of open-weight models with a massive 405B parameter version that rivals the best proprietary models in reasoning and knowledge.

papers.title: Accurate structure prediction of biomolecular interactions with AlphaFold 3
papers.description: Introduced AlphaFold 3, capable of predicting the structure and interactions of all life's molecules, including proteins, DNA, RNA, and ligands, with unprecedented accuracy.

papers.title: OpenAI o1 System Card
papers.description: Introduced a new class of models trained with reinforcement learning to perform complex reasoning via chain-of-thought before responding.

papers.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.description: Demonstrated that high-level reasoning capabilities can be achieved in open-weights models through pure Reinforcement Learning (RL) without massive supervised fine-tuning.
key_contributions: Open-weight reasoning model matching o1 performance

papers.title: ArithMotion: Intent-Aware Lifelike VR Avatars on Mobile
papers.description: Developed a mobile-ready system that allows VR avatars to express socially-aligned motions by understanding user intent, enabling lifelike nonverbal communication.

papers.title: NVIDIA DiffusionRenderer: Unifying Inverse and Forward Rendering
papers.description: Introduced a neural rendering technique that unifies rendering passes, enabling AI-powered lighting control and photorealistic scene transformation.

papers.title: Perceptually Guided 3DGS Streaming and Rendering for Virtual Reality
papers.description: Optimized 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) for VR streaming, focusing on perceptual quality and real-time performance in immersive environments.
key_contributions: Real-time VR streaming for neural volumes
